resources.string: HyTalkPTT is off in Settings → Accessibility. Turn it on for side/programmable PTT keys. If Bluetooth PTT still shows nothing below, your mic may use a non-standard protocol (e.g. SPP only) that Android does not expose as keys.

Press the device side PTT key (shown here via keyboard events). Bluetooth: only works if the phone delivers headset or media keys to apps (HFP hook / AVRCP). Many dedicated PTT mics (e.g. some Inrico models) use a private protocol — then nothing appears in logcat (tag HyTalkPTT-MediaBtn) and you must use the device’s own PTT key only. BLE: dedicated button PTT-Z01 — enable the BLE checkbox, Search, connect, then Save (see README).
Bluetooth headset / microphone PTT (HFP hook, AVRCP play/pause)
Bluetooth SPP (RFCOMM serial) — e.g. Inrico B02PTT-FF01 (+PTT=P / +PTT=R)
Turn on the button, grant Bluetooth scan/connect (Android 12+) and location if requested, then Search. After connect, tap Save. The app reconnects on startup when this option is saved on.
BLE PTT connected. Tap Save to store the device.
BLE connect failed (status %1$d). If the button is connected in another app (e.g. BLE Scanner), disconnect it there and retry.
BLE button is busy in another app (status %1$d). Disconnect it in BLE Scanner and retry.
Device name is not PTT-Z01 — disconnected
Bluetooth is off or permissions missing (scan / connect / location)
BLE permissions: grant Location (Fine or Coarse)
BLE permissions: Nearby devices granted, but Location is off/denied (some devices require it for scan)

Pair the device in system Bluetooth settings first. SPP uses the standard serial UUID; we connect to a bonded Inrico B02PTT* device if present, otherwise the first bonded device. On Android 12+ grant the Bluetooth connect permission. Unknown bytes are shown in a toast so you can map new headsets.

Important: enable “Bluetooth headset / microphone PTT” and/or “BLE PTT button (PTT-Z01)” if you use them, then tap Save. Until you Save, those sources are not active after you leave this screen (see log: Bluetooth PTT OFF).

Huawei often sends headset PLAY/PAUSE to com.android.mediacenter (Huawei Music), not to other apps — system log: “Sending KeyEvent … to com.android.mediacenter”. Force-stop or disable Huawei Music / Media center, enable Bluetooth PTT + Save, then try again. Dedicated PTT on some mics never appears in system logs — use the phone side key. Diagnose: adb logcat | grep -i MediaSessionService
AVRCP Play (MEDIA_PLAY) triggers PTT — uncheck if the headset power / hook button falsely keys PTT (same key code as many “play” buttons).
Tap to toggle transmit (for radios/headsets that only send a short PLAY/PAUSE pulse — first tap = PTT on, second tap = off)
Enable at least one PTT source

Scroll screen…

Other accessibility services may interfere with global key capture. For reliable PTT, enable only HyTalkPTT under Settings → Accessibility (turn off any others you do not need).

To use HyTalkPTT, do this in order:

1) Accessibility: tap Configure accessibility — Settings → Accessibility → HyTalkPTT → enable the toggle
2) Configure PTT key: tap Configure PTT key — press your PTT button; enable Bluetooth PTT, SPP, and/or BLE PTT-Z01 if needed, then Save settings
3) Configure programmable keys (Motorola): Settings → Programmable keys → Select PTT Key app → HyTalkPTT
Could not open PTT key settings
